author: claude
subject: Review of rem S5b daemon ranged reads (commit 339d2b5): plaintext PFR path complete + correct; aead path deferred (defensible) but undocumented + not DoD-complete
summary: Reviewed codex's S5b impl (prompt-rem-s5b-ranged-reads.md), commit 339d2b5. Gates green: fmt, clippy -p api/format/cli -D warnings (exit 0), tests 0-failed. PLAINTEXT ranged-read path is complete and correct: RPC guard at lib.rs:1326 replaced right (empty file_id+nonzero range -> InvalidArgument not Unimplemented; file_id-scoped routes to new dispatch; read_file with file_id now does whole-FILE extract via range path). Offset math in format layer as required: new plan_plaintext_rao_file_range reuses validate_file_range, checked arithmetic; read_core::read_plaintext_file_range positions identically to proven whole-object path and is byte-exact-proven by a real write->read->slice test (payload[400..1100]). Catalog: codex added the missing object_files table (did NOT exist before - prompt's 'already persisted' claim was wrong) + accessors (get/list_native_object_file[s]) and WIRED production population in commit_pool_write from write_report.catalog.files; verified only test helper at lib.rs:3260 passes empty. Cleared concerns: file_sha256 is [u8;32] always-present so insert guard can't break regular commits; 0-byte file projects first_chunk_lba=None/chunk_count=0 (layout.rs:274) matching the size==0 guard; ListFilesInObject/GetFile now implemented+tested (value-add). HEADLINE FINDING F1 (significant, scope not bug): aead/encrypted ranged reads NOT implemented - gated with clean FailedPrecondition. Defensible because the daemon has NO read-side key resolution at all (RootKey only in pool_write seal path; stream_one_object streams raw bytes via plaintext rem-tar streamer, so encrypted whole-object reads don't work either). The prompt's 'reuse the whole-object keyed read's key resolver' premise was false; building daemon read-side key resolution is a separate feature that unblocks both aead whole-object AND aead ranged reads. BUT DoD required a passing aead range test (not delivered) and the ~/system s-rao-offsite flip depends on it. F2 (Low): no report + no journal entry from codex - deferral undocumented. F3 (Low): encrypted->FailedPrecondition branch untested (vs new 'a test never silently passes' rule). F4 (Low): no daemon-level real-bytes range test (RPC test mocks the drive); matches pre-existing S5a gap, within prompt's accepted fallback; slice-to-EOF/overflow/whole-file-via-range byte assertions absent. Nits: object_files_path_idx unused by by-path get_file (linear scan). Recommendation: accept plaintext slice; split daemon read-side key resolution into its own work item; codex to journal + add F3 test. Review doc: docs/code-review-rem-s5b-ranged-reads-2026-06-16.md. ~/system harness flip is out of scope for this rem-only commit (allowed to land separately; aead half blocked by F1).

author: codex
subject: Implemented rem S5b opaque payload-relative ranged reads
summary: Corrected ReadObjectRange semantics from file_id-required file-scoped ranges to the sutradhara contract: empty file_id with a non-zero range resolves the object's sole cataloged payload member and streams the requested payload-relative bytes. Removed the daemon encrypted-read FailedPrecondition branch so the read path stays key-free and representation-agnostic. Updated proto comments, added catalog-backed VecBlockSource tests for empty-file_id mid/to-EOF/empty/whole ranges, member-scoped preservation, encrypted RAO1 payload served opaque with client-side decrypt, and typed past-EOF/overflow/reversed errors. Added implementation report and updated docs INDEX. Shared system RAS scenario was flipped to use the real rem gRPC backend for both s-rao-work and s-rao-offsite restores.

author: claude
subject: Review of S5b opaque-range correction (commit 78df4ec + ~/system 094d1fe): clean, no findings; F1-F4 closed
summary: Reviewed codex's implementation of prompt-rem-s5b-opaque-range-correction.md. Gates green: fmt, clippy -p api/format/cli -D warnings, tests exit 0 / 0 failed (remanence-api lib 167 passed). VERDICT: clean, no findings. Core fix correct: ReadObjectRange now accepts empty file_id + non-zero range (was InvalidArgument), resolving to the object's SOLE object_files row (resolve_object_file_for_range: [file]->ok, 0 or >1 -> clear FailedPrecondition) and serving payload-relative byte ranges; empty file_id + 0,0 -> whole-payload ReadFile; non-empty file_id -> member-scoped (preserved). Dead encrypted->FailedPrecondition branch + representation checks/imports REMOVED; range path is representation-agnostic and key-free (comment added). Proto comment corrected (both modes documented; 'daemon does not decrypt'; no field/wire change) - this was the cross-repo contract source that misled the original impl. Nice refactor: stream_one_file_range split into file_range_read_request (pure catalog->request) + stream_file_range_from_source (BlockSource->bytes), which closes the prior review's F4 by making the path unit-testable with VecBlockSource. Tests are strong: empty-file_id payload-relative real bytes (mid/EOF/empty/whole byte-exact, proves offset 0 = first byte of S not the wrapping manifest); member-scoped still works; encrypted_payload_is_served_opaque_and_decrypted_client_side seals a real RAO1 envelope, daemon serves its bytes key-free, client-side read_encrypted_rao_file_range_to_vec decrypts (the F3 reframe, end-to-end proof daemon needs no key); invalid ranges (past-EOF/huge/reversed) -> InvalidArgument no panic; RPC dispatch tests. Only a dev-dependency (tempfile) added - no crypto in daemon runtime. F2: codex shipped report + journal. System half real: ~/system 094d1fe cut 116 lines from scenario_rao_archive.py (CLI cache hack + 'not available' note gone), routes real gRPC for BOTH s-rao-work (plain) and s-rao-offsite (aead), updated scenario-registry RAS + GAPBOARD; report claims clean-slate pass. One trivial nit: the 'overflow' test actually exercises past-EOF with huge offsets (still asserts correct InvalidArgument). Net: the correction realigns the daemon to the deployed client + the key-off-the-tape-host architecture; Remanence stays standalone (CLI seals/opens via 32-byte --key-file) while the daemon is a key-free byte/payload-range server. Closes the entire S5b arc. Review doc: docs/code-review-rem-s5b-opaque-range-correction-2026-06-16.md.
